dataset: Synthetic CT chest phantom generated by pydicom in setup_task.sh

pi_items.source: Confirmed via live testing: directory exists, owned by ga:ga, permissions 755, user ga can write files to it.
Path matches task description and export_result.sh checks. Also accepts .jpg/.jpeg extensions.
Path matches task description and export_result.sh parsing logic.
Live testing confirmed 40 DICOM files (slice_000.dcm to slice_039.dcm, 525KB each) in this directory, owned by ga:ga.

Computed from phantom code: cardiac_semi_x = 22 + 40*exp(-0.5*((s-16)/7)^2). True peak at s=16 (InstanceNumber=17, diameter=84.3mm). InstanceNumber 13 (s=12) has diameter=76.1mm. Range 13-19 correctly brackets the peak. Acceptance is generous but appropriate since adjacent slices differ by only ~2mm.
InstanceNumber 19 (s=18) has diameter=82.1mm, still clearly in the peak region. The true peak InstanceNumber=17 is well within [13,19].

True peak diameter is 84.3mm. The minimum acceptable of 55mm is generous, allowing for the agent measuring a slightly different structure or partial measurement. Live testing confirmed the line measurement tool returns values in mm correctly (measured 107.5mm for a wider line drawn across body contour).
True peak diameter is 84.3mm. Maximum of 125mm allows for the agent's line measurement extending slightly beyond the structure boundary. Generous but appropriate.

Cardiac structure nominal HU=55 with Gaussian noise sigma=8. Live testing measured ROI Mean=55.4 HU, Std=7.4 HU on the actual data. If the agent places the ROI partially overlapping body contour (HU=0), mean could drop to ~30-40 HU. Floor of 15 HU accounts for significant ROI misplacement.

Cardiac structure nominal HU=55. With noise and edge effects, an ROI entirely within the structure should read ~47-63 HU. Ceiling of 95 HU is generous but guards against the agent measuring a mixed region.
Vertebral body nominal HU=280 with noise sigma=8. Live testing measured ROI Mean=280.9 HU, Std=7.3 HU. Floor of 150 allows for partial ROI overlap with surrounding body tissue (HU=0).
Vertebral body nominal HU=280. Ceiling of 450 is generous. A well-placed ROI should read ~272-288 HU.
True soft-tissue-to-bone ratio = 55/280 = 0.196. Floor of 0.03 is very generous, allowing for extreme measurement imprecision.
True ratio = 0.196. Ceiling of 0.80 catches inverted ratio computations (bone/soft_tissue = 5.09 would fail). Appropriate.

Cardiac structure HU=55. Per the classification thresholds in the task description: fat (<-30), water (-30 to 30), soft tissue (30 to 100), dense (>100). Since 30 < 55 < 100, classification is 'soft tissue'. Confirmed via both code analysis and live ROI measurement (Mean=55.4 HU).
Contextual framing — tissue density characterization with ROI analysis and W/L window comparison is a standard radiology workflow.
Contextual — Weasis is a DICOM medical imaging viewer.

All features verified available in live testing: scroll via mouse wheel changes frames, line measurement tool draws with mm readout, ellipse ROI with Pixel Statistics shows Mean/StdDev/Min/Max, layout selector offers 1x2 Views, W/L presets include Bone/Lung/Abdomen/Mediastinum plus manual input, File>Export>Exporting view saves PNG/JPEG, terminal available for writing text files.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses a synthetic 40-slice CT chest phantom generated by pydicom (PatientName: QAPhantom^Chest, PatientID: QA-TISSUE-001). The phantom contains: a body contour (HU=0), bilateral lungs (HU=-850), a central cardiac-like soft-tissue structure (HU=55, centered at pixel col=235 row=250), a vertebral body (HU=280, centered at col=256 row=375, radius=17px), a descending aorta (HU=45), and Gaussian noise with sigma=8 HU. The cardiac structure's transverse size follows a Gaussian profile peaking at slice index s=16 (DICOM InstanceNumber=17, Weasis Frame [17]), where its transverse diameter is 84.3mm. The adjacent Frame [16] is nearly identical at 83.8mm. PixelSpacing is 0.68mm/pixel. A well-placed elliptical ROI on the cardiac structure should read Mean ~55 HU with StdDev ~7-8 HU. A well-placed ROI on the vertebral body should read Mean ~280 HU with StdDev ~7-8 HU. The correct soft-tissue-to-bone HU ratio is approximately 0.196 (=55/280). Since the cardiac structure mean HU of ~55 falls in the range 30-100, the correct tissue classification is 'soft tissue'. The agent must: (1) scroll through 40 slices to find the peak cardiac slice (Frame 16 or 17), (2) draw a line measurement across the cardiac structure (~84mm), (3) set up a 1x2 side-by-side layout with soft tissue window W:350 L:50 on the left and bone window W:2000 L:300 on the right, (4) place elliptical ROIs and read pixel statistics from the 'Selected' section in the Draw & Measure panel (not the image overlay), (5) export the dual-window view as density_analysis.png, and (6) write density_report.txt with all measured values. The ROI pixel statistics in Weasis appear in the 'Selected' panel at the bottom of the 'Draw & Measure' sidebar when the 'Pixel Statistics' checkbox is enabled, showing Mean, StdDev, Min, Max, Median, Pixels, Area, and other statistics. The layout selector is a grid-shaped toolbar button that opens a dropdown with 1x1, 1x2, 2x1, etc. layout options.
